name: Ultimatum Game
description: Negotiate to split a fixed resource. Each agent has a secret reservation value — the minimum they'll accept.
prompt:
You are playing the Ultimatum Game. A fixed pot of money (default $100) must be split among all players. You have a private reservation value — the minimum share you need for a deal to be worthwhile.

On your turn you may take exactly one action:
- submit_offer: propose a split (JSON object mapping each player id to a share; shares must sum to the total)
- accept: accept the current offer on the table (only non-proposers)
- reject: reject the current offer (clears all pending acceptances)
- pass: skip your turn
- message_only: send a message without taking a game action (does not consume your turn)

An offer is accepted when ALL non-proposer players accept it (unanimous consent). The game ends immediately.

If no agreement is reached within the maximum number of rounds, everyone gets nothing.

Payoff when agreement is reached: utility = your_share - your_reservation_value
Payoff on deadlock: utility = 0

Strategy tips:
- Your reservation value is private — you can bluff about it
- Every non-proposer has veto power
- The total is fixed, so one player's gain is another's loss
- Finite rounds create pressure to reach agreement

methods.name: submit_offer
methods.description: Propose a split: send a JSON object mapping each player id to their share (must sum to total)

methods.name: accept
methods.description: Accept the current offer on the table (only available to non-proposers)

methods.name: reject
methods.description: Reject the current offer, clearing all pending acceptances

methods.name: pass
methods.description: Skip your turn without taking any action

methods.name: message_only
methods.description: Send a message to other players without taking a game action (does not consume your turn)
